Title: Evolutionary history of magnoliid genomes and benzylisoquinoline alkaloid biosynthesis

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms upstream intermediate toward (S)-reticuline and downstream BIAs
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Benzylisoquinoline alkaloids (including aristolochic acid I-related BIA pathway intermediates)
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes conversion of (S)-norcoclaurine to (S)-coclaurine; strong catalytic activity in transient assay
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Identified as a 6OMT candidate by phylogeny, catalytic residues, and expression pattern. 2. Transient expression in N. benthamiana showed strong conversion of (S)-norcoclaurine to (S)-coclaurine. 3. Supports conservation of the shared-upstream BIA pathway in A. manshuriensis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Phylogenetic analysis; residue comparison; expression analysis; transient expression in N. benthamiana; LC-MS metabolite detection
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: One of two 6OMT candidates; showed stronger activity than Am04G071000. Located on Aman4, a conserved chromosomal block derived from AMK7.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Selected as candidate based on expression pattern in A. manshuriensis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently expressed in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Substrate feeding of (S)-norcoclaurine in infiltrated N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate enzyme for reconstructing shared-upstream BIA biosynthesis in heterologous systems or metabolic engineering

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms upstream intermediate toward (S)-reticuline and downstream BIAs
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Catalyzes conversion of (S)-norcoclaurine to (S)-coclaurine with weak activity
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Identified as a second 6OMT candidate. 2. Transient expression in N. benthamiana showed weak catalytic activity toward (S)-coclaurine formation. 3. Likely retained after recent WGD and may have diverged functionally.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Phylogenetic analysis; expression analysis; transient expression in N. benthamiana; LC-MS metabolite detection
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Homologous to Am02G209000 after recent WGD; paper suggests sub-functionalization after duplication. Located on Aman4 from AMK7.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Selected as candidate based on expression pattern in A. manshuriensis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently expressed in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Substrate feeding of (S)-norcoclaurine in infiltrated N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful auxiliary 6OMT allele for pathway engineering and study of post-WGD subfunctionalization

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms direct intermediate toward (S)-reticuline and downstream BIAs
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Identified as the functional CNMT among two CNMT candidates. 2. Transient expression in N. benthamiana converted (S)-coclaurine to (S)-N-methylcoclaurine. 3. Structural and phylogenetic evidence support membership in the functional CNMT-I clade.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Phylogenetic analysis; structural comparison with TfCNMT; domain/residue analysis; transient expression in N. benthamiana; LC-MS metabolite detection
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Located on Aman5 from conserved AMK10. Retained CNMT function after WGD, whereas paralog Am03G024800 lost the CMAS domain and function.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Selected as candidate based on expression pattern in A. manshuriensis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently expressed in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Substrate feeding of (S)-coclaurine in infiltrated N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important validated step for reconstituting shared-upstream BIA biosynthesis

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms direct intermediate toward (S)-reticuline and downstream BIAs
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Identified as the sole CYP80B/NMCH candidate in A. manshuriensis. 2. Transient expression in N. benthamiana converted (S)-N-methylcoclaurine to (S)-3'-hydroxy-N-methylcoclaurine. 3. Supports presence of a conserved shared-upstream BIA pathway in magnoliids.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Phylogenetic analysis; expression analysis; transient expression in N. benthamiana; LC-MS metabolite detection
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Located on Aman4 from conserved AMK7.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Selected as candidate based on expression pattern in A. manshuriensis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently expressed in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Substrate feeding of (S)-N-methylcoclaurine in infiltrated N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Core oxidative step for pathway reconstruction in BIA engineering
